title: DHT Classroom Station
subtitle: Temperature & Humidity Monitoring System
overview: In this session, students learn how to build a DHT Classroom Station using Arduino UNO and the DHT11 sensor. Students will understand temperature and humidity monitoring, sensor working principles, and how smart classrooms use environmental monitoring systems.

images._note: Add actual classroom station photos, DHT sensor wiring, Arduino setup, student activity photos and monitoring screen images.

activity.title: Build a DHT Classroom Monitoring Station
steps: Place the DHT11 sensor on the breadboard
Connect GND pin to Arduino GND
Connect DATA pin to Arduino Digital Pin 2
Upload the DHT sensor code using Arduino IDE
Open Serial Monitor to view temperature and humidity
Observe changes in readings by breathing near the sensor

quiz.q: What does the DHT11 sensor measure?
quiz.explanation: The DHT11 sensor measures temperature and humidity from the environment.

options: Amount of water vapor in air
quiz.explanation: Humidity means the amount of water vapor present in the air.

quiz.q: Which board is used as the brain of the project?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO acts as the microcontroller and controls the project.

quiz.q: Which software is used to upload code into Arduino?
quiz.explanation: Arduino IDE is used to write and upload programs to Arduino boards.

quiz.q: Which pin of DHT11 sends data to Arduino?
quiz.explanation: The DATA pin sends sensor readings to Arduino.

quiz.q: What is the full form of IoT?
quiz.explanation: IoT stands for Internet of Things.

quiz.q: What happens when you breathe near the DHT11 sensor?
quiz.explanation: Breathing near the sensor increases moisture in the air, increasing humidity readings.

quiz.q: Which monitor shows sensor readings in Arduino IDE?
quiz.explanation: Serial Monitor displays live sensor data from Arduino.

quiz.q: What type of device is DHT11?
quiz.explanation: DHT11 is a sensor because it collects environmental data.

quiz.q: Where can DHT classroom stations be useful?
quiz.explanation: DHT monitoring systems are useful in classrooms, weather stations and greenhouses.
